symptom: Toaster lever won't stay down or pops back up immediately

preconditions: Let it cool if recently used
hazards: Electric shock risk if plugged in

steps.what_to_look_for: Power cord disconnected from wall outlet

steps.action: Plug the toaster back in and try the lever again — note: the lever needs power to latch
steps.what_to_look_for: The electromagnet inside needs current to hold the lever down. If it still won't stay, the circuit is broken
steps.visual_confirmation: Lever either latches (working) or immediately pops back (circuit broken)
steps.safety_note: Only plug in for this test, then unplug immediately after

steps.action: Remove crumb tray and shake out all debris — crumbs can jam the mechanism
steps.what_to_look_for: Excessive crumbs caught in the lever mechanism or around the bread carriage
steps.visual_confirmation: Mechanism area is clear, lever moves freely up and down

steps.action: If the lever still won't latch with power, test the thermal fuse for continuity
steps.what_to_look_for: The thermal fuse is in series with the electromagnet. If blown, no current flows and the magnet can't hold
steps.visual_confirmation: Multimeter shows continuity through the thermal fuse
steps.if_issue_found: Blown thermal fuse = most common cause. Replace with exact same rated fuse. This is a safety device — never bypass it

steps.action: Check the latch mechanism for broken or bent parts
steps.what_to_look_for: The metal latch tab that hooks onto the electromagnet may be bent or broken. The spring that returns the lever should be intact
steps.visual_confirmation: Latch tab is straight and engages properly, spring has tension
steps.if_issue_found: Bent latch can sometimes be carefully bent back. Broken latch = replace the toaster

steps.action: After fixing, plug in and test the lever
steps.what_to_look_for: Lever should push down, click, and hold. Elements should begin to glow
steps.visual_confirmation: Lever latches, elements heat up, lever releases when done
steps.safety_note: Watch the first full cycle to confirm proper operation
